title: Parallels-graph trio: commentarial-load vs parallel-density, relation-type stratigraphy, and coverage cartography

hypotheses.h1: HOUSE THESIS (canon supple, commentary systematizes). Across the three structural sub-studies sharing the parallels graph: (A) within each mula collection (DN/MN/SN/AN, never pooled), per-root-sutta commentarial load (attha_chars/root_chars) is POSITIVELY associated with parallel density (non-pli parallels per root Mchar) AFTER partialling out root tag-richness and (proxy for) cataloguing granularity — i.e. the suttas the tradition chose to gloss most heavily are also the suttas most embedded in the cross-tradition transmission web, reflecting their canonical centrality rather than length. (B) RetellShare = (retells+mentions)/all-edges, computed per collection at fixed stratum and length-controlled, is ordered DN > MN > {SN, AN}, with SN being parallels-dominant (its edges are overwhelmingly verbatim 'parallels', the anchor of the gradient): long discourse collections carry proportionally more loose narrative re-tellings/mentions, short pericope collections carry proportionally more tight verbatim parallels. (C) Coverage/density/breadth/relation-mix differ by stratum and work, with the early canon (1early) showing higher external parallel coverage than late (2late) — BUT this ordering must be carried by the FOREIGN graph and must NOT be reproduced by the PLI-only intra-Pali sub-graph (negative control); relation_type is an edge label that escapes the stratum-by-construction trap.
hypotheses.h0: PRE-COMMITTED NULL (reported if it wins). (A) Once root tag-richness and cataloguing-granularity (distinct-work/sutta granularity proxy) are partialled out, the within-collection partial Spearman between comm_load and par_density is indistinguishable from zero in every collection (|rho| < 0.15, CI crosses 0), i.e. both quantities are just shadows of root length / register and carry no shared canonical-centrality signal. (B) RetellShare does not follow DN > MN > {SN,AN}; the apparent ordering, if any, collapses once edges are length-normalized and stratum-fixed (the gradient is a length artifact, not a register/redaction signal), OR SN is not parallels-dominant. (C) The early>late external-coverage ordering IS reproduced by the PLI-only intra-Pali sub-graph at comparable effect size — meaning the gradient is an artifact of SuttaCentral cataloguing completeness / corpus-mapping density, not of genuine differential cross-tradition transmission, and the early/late 'coverage' claim is not interpretable as transmission history.

frozen_lexica.name: COMM_METHOD
frozen_lexica.role: Sub-study (A) content check: textual markers of commentarial systematization/glossing method in attha/tika passages. Used to confirm that high comm_load passages are actually doing exegetical work (word-analysis, synonym-listing, meaning-extraction) and not merely long. NOT a numerator; a descriptive/qualitative co-pass. Senses must be blind-coded (pariyāya is polysemous: 'method/discourse-mode' vs 'occasion' vs 'synonym').
frozen_lexica.notes: vevacana = synonym-gloss (commentary signature). pariyāya/pariyaya = exegetical 'method/synonym' AND canonical 'discourse mode'/'occasion' — POLYSEMOUS, sense-audit required. nirutti/nibbacana = etymological word-analysis (CST shows both forms). atthuddhāra/atthuddhara = meaning-extraction/topic-summary. padabhājana/padabhajana = word-by-word breakdown (Vibhanga signature). vibhaṅga/vibhanga = analysis. adhippāya/adhippaya = intended-meaning gloss. lakkhaṇa/lakkhana = defining-characteristic. ASCII-fallback alternates (no-diacritic) included because CST ingests sometimes drop the macron/dot on ingest.

frozen_lexica.name: DHAMMA_EPITHET
frozen_lexica.role: Sub-study (A) canonical-centrality marker in MULA roots: the svākkhāta/sandiṭṭhika/akālika/ehipassika/opanayika/paccattaṃ formula and surrounding Dhamma-quality epithets mark the high-prestige canonical core. Used to test whether comm_load tracks these prestige roots independently of length. Descriptive co-pass only.
frozen_lexica.notes: CONFIRMED variant pair opaneyyik|opanayik (SuttaCentral lists both). svākkhāta/svakkhata, sandiṭṭhika/sanditthika, akālika/akalika, ehipassika/ehipassiko all carry CST-vs-SC macron/retroflex-dot variation; ASCII-fallback forms included. paccattaṃ veditabbo viññūhi closes the formula. A wrong-diacritic single-spelling regex here returns zero — that is the failure mode this lexicon guards against.

frozen_lexica.name: NEG_REGISTER_CONTROL
frozen_lexica.role: NEGATIVE CONTROL lexicon for sub-study (A): high-frequency narrative/framing/connective vocabulary that pervades BOTH canon and commentary and should be FLAT across comm_load (no association with parallel density beyond length). If par_density correlates with these generic-register terms as strongly as with COMM_METHOD/DHAMMA_EPITHET, the (A) effect is a generic length/register artifact, not a systematization signal.
frozen_lexica.notes: evaṃ me sutaṃ / tena samayena / atha kho / bhagavā = the universal nidāna+narration register. evaṃ|evam and bhagavā|bhagava carry the anusvāra/macron CST-SC split. These are deliberately content-empty framing tokens: present everywhere, so any correlation they show with par_density is pure length/register and calibrates the real lexica's signal against it.

enumeration_sql.label: A0_root_universe_per_sutta
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Sub-study (A) unit table: one row per MULA root sutta (SC fine rows, deduped), with root_chars and tag-richness. Restrict to SC edition to get per-sutta granularity (CST is coarse vagga rows). DN/MN/SN/AN kept separate via collection prefix; NEVER pooled downstream.
WITH root AS (
  SELECT p.id, p.work_slug, char_length(p.original) AS root_chars,
         CASE WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' THEN 'DN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' THEN 'MN'
              WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' THEN 'SN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'an%' THEN 'AN' ELSE 'OTHER' END AS coll
  FROM passages p
  WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug <> 'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
    AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late')
)
SELECT r.id, r.work_slug, r.coll, r.root_chars,
       (SELECT count(*) FROM passage_tags t WHERE t.passage_id=r.id) AS tag_richness
FROM root r
WHERE r.coll IN ('DN','MN','SN','AN')
ORDER BY r.coll, r.work_slug;

enumeration_sql.label: A1_par_density_per_root
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Per-root parallel density numerator: non-pli (foreign) parallel edges per root. Joined per-Mchar downstream against A0.root_chars. relation_type retained for later mix.
SELECT pp.passage_id,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang <> 'pli') AS foreign_par_edges,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang = 'pli') AS pli_par_edges,
       count(*) AS all_edges,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type='parallels') AS rt_parallels,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type='retells')  AS rt_retells,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type='mentions')  AS rt_mentions
FROM passage_parallels pp
WHERE pp.passage_id IN (
   SELECT p.id FROM passages p
   WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
     AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late')
     AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%'))
GROUP BY pp.passage_id;

enumeration_sql.label: A2_comm_load_per_root
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Per-root commentarial load: attha chars whose commentary maps to this root sutta. Mapping is via shared work_slug base (mula 'mn1' <-> attha 'mn1-attha' / 'pli-vism' excluded as it is whole-work). comm_load = attha_chars / root_chars computed downstream.
SELECT base.root_id, base.work_slug AS root_slug,
       COALESCE(SUM(char_length(a.original)),0) AS attha_chars,
       count(a.id) AS attha_passages
FROM (
   SELECT p.id AS root_id, p.work_slug,
          regexp_replace(p.work_slug,'^([a-z]+[0-9]+).*$','\1') AS slug_key
   FROM passages p
   WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
     AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late')
     AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%')
) base
LEFT JOIN passages a
   ON a.work_role='attha' AND a.is_primary=true
  AND regexp_replace(a.work_slug,'^([a-z]+[0-9]+).*$','\1') = base.slug_key
GROUP BY base.root_id, base.work_slug;

enumeration_sql.label: A3_comm_method_density_attha
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Sub-study (A) content co-pass: COMM_METHOD lexicon density per Mchar in attha layer (denominator attha Mchar = 29.488533). Confirms high-comm_load attha actually does exegesis. Hits returned per work_slug for the qualitative pass; magnitude always per-Mchar.
SELECT 'COMM_METHOD' AS lexicon,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE a.original ~* '(\mvevacan|\mpariyāy|\mpariyay|\mnirutti|\mnibbacan|\matthuddhār|\matthuddhar|\mpadabhājan|\mpadabhajan|\mvibhaṅg|\mvibhang|\madhippāy|\madhippay|\mlakkhaṇ|\mlakkhan)') AS hit_passages,
       SUM(char_length(a.original)) AS attha_total_chars,
       round( count(*) FILTER (WHERE a.original ~* '(\mvevacan|\mpariyāy|\mpariyay|\mnirutti|\mnibbacan|\matthuddhār|\matthuddhar|\mpadabhājan|\mpadabhajan|\mvibhaṅg|\mvibhang|\madhippāy|\madhippay|\mlakkhaṇ|\mlakkhan)')::numeric / 29.488533, 3) AS hits_per_Mchar_attha
FROM passages a
WHERE a.work_role='attha' AND a.is_primary=true;

enumeration_sql.label: A4_neg_register_density_mula
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- NEGATIVE CONTROL pass: generic-register lexicon density per mula Mchar (denominator 53.543921) and DHAMMA_EPITHET prestige density, side by side, for the (A) artifact check. If par_density tracks NEG_REGISTER as strongly as DHAMMA_EPITHET, (A) is a length/register artifact.
SELECT lex, hit_passages,
       round(hit_passages::numeric / 53.543921, 3) AS hits_per_Mchar_mula
FROM (
  SELECT 'NEG_REGISTER' AS lex,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '(\mevaṃ\M|\mevam\M|\mtena\M|\msamayena|\mbhagavā\M|\mbhagava\M|\matha\M|\mkho\M|\mahosi|\mvuccati)') AS hit_passages
  FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'DHAMMA_EPITHET',
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE p.original ~* '(\msvākkhāt|\msvakkhat|\msandiṭṭhik|\msanditthik|\makālik|\makalik|\mehipassik|\mopaneyyik|\mopanayik|\mpaccattaṃ|\mpaccatta)')
  FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
) s;

enumeration_sql.label: B1_retellshare_by_collection
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Sub-study (B): RetellShare = (retells+mentions)/all-edges, per collection, stratum-fixed to 1early, on MULA roots only, with edge-count and char totals so it can be length-controlled downstream. SN parallels-dominance read from rt_parallels share.
WITH e AS (
  SELECT CASE WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' THEN 'DN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' THEN 'MN'
              WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' THEN 'SN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'an%' THEN 'AN' END AS coll,
         pp.relation_type, char_length(p.original) AS rc
  FROM passages p JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=p.id
  WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
    AND stratum(p.work_slug)='1early'
    AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%')
)
SELECT coll,
       count(*) AS all_edges,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type IN ('retells','mentions')) AS retell_mention_edges,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type='parallels') AS parallels_edges,
       round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type IN ('retells','mentions'))::numeric / NULLIF(count(*),0),4) AS retell_share,
       round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE relation_type='parallels')::numeric / NULLIF(count(*),0),4) AS parallels_share,
       SUM(DISTINCT rc) AS approx_char_basis
FROM e WHERE coll IS NOT NULL GROUP BY coll ORDER BY retell_share DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: B2_retellshare_length_control_bins
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Sub-study (B) length control: same RetellShare split into per-collection root-length terciles, so the DN>MN>{SN,AN} ordering can be checked WITHIN comparable length bins (defends against 'DN just has long suttas' artifact / H0-B).
WITH base AS (
  SELECT p.id, char_length(p.original) AS rc,
         CASE WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' THEN 'DN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' THEN 'MN'
              WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' THEN 'SN' WHEN p.work_slug LIKE 'an%' THEN 'AN' END AS coll
  FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
    AND stratum(p.work_slug)='1early'
    AND (p.work_slug LIKE 'dn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'mn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'sn%' OR p.work_slug LIKE 'an%')
), binned AS (
  SELECT id, coll, ntile(3) OVER (PARTITION BY coll ORDER BY rc) AS len_tercile FROM base
)
SELECT b.coll, b.len_tercile,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type IN ('retells','mentions')) AS rm_edges,
       count(pp.*) AS all_edges,
       round(count(*) FILTER (WHERE pp.relation_type IN ('retells','mentions'))::numeric / NULLIF(count(pp.*),0),4) AS retell_share
FROM binned b LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=b.id
GROUP BY b.coll, b.len_tercile ORDER BY b.coll, b.len_tercile;

enumeration_sql.label: C1_coverage_cartography_by_stratum
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Sub-study (C): per stratum, external-parallel COVERAGE (share of mula passages with >=1 foreign edge), DENSITY (foreign edges per stratum Mchar), BREADTH (distinct parallel_lang per stratum), RELATION-MIX. Per-character throughout (T2). Stratum is a lookup label (T1): never read a layer disagreement as independent chronology.
WITH m AS (
  SELECT p.id, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, char_length(p.original) AS rc
  FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
)
SELECT m.strat,
       count(DISTINCT m.id) AS mula_passages,
       count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang <> 'pli') AS passages_with_foreign,
       round(count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli')::numeric / NULLIF(count(DISTINCT m.id),0),4) AS foreign_coverage,
       SUM(m.rc)/1000000.0 AS strat_Mchar,
       round(count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli')::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(DISTINCT m.rc)/1000000.0,0),3) AS foreign_edges_per_Mchar,
       count(DISTINCT pp.parallel_lang) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') AS lang_breadth
FROM m LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=m.id
WHERE m.strat IN ('1early','2late')
GROUP BY m.strat ORDER BY m.strat;

enumeration_sql.label: C2_NEGCONTROL_pli_only_coverage
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- LEAD NEGATIVE CONTROL for (C): the SAME coverage cartography but on the PLI-ONLY (intra-Pali) sub-graph. The early>late ordering must NOT be reproduced here. If intra-Pali coverage shows the same early>late gradient, the (C) effect is SC cataloguing-completeness artifact, not transmission history (H0-C wins).
WITH m AS (
  SELECT p.id, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, char_length(p.original) AS rc
  FROM passages p WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
)
SELECT m.strat,
       count(DISTINCT m.id) AS mula_passages,
       count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang='pli') AS passages_with_pli,
       round(count(DISTINCT m.id) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang='pli')::numeric / NULLIF(count(DISTINCT m.id),0),4) AS pli_coverage,
       round(count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang='pli')::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(DISTINCT m.rc)/1000000.0,0),3) AS pli_edges_per_Mchar
FROM m LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=m.id
WHERE m.strat IN ('1early','2late')
GROUP BY m.strat ORDER BY m.strat;

enumeration_sql.label: C3_sc_mapping_completeness_bound
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- (C) SC-mapping-completeness bound: edition double-ingest check. Counts distinct works/suttas per stratum under SC (fine per-sutta) vs CST (coarse vagga) so coverage denominators are honest. A coverage 'gap' that is really a cataloguing-granularity gap gets flagged here, NOT called an interpolation.
SELECT stratum(work_slug) AS strat,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='sc') AS sc_rows,
       count(*) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='cst') AS cst_rows,
       count(DISTINCT work_slug) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='sc') AS sc_distinct_works,
       count(DISTINCT work_slug) FILTER (WHERE source_edition='cst') AS cst_distinct_works
FROM passages WHERE work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism'
GROUP BY stratum(work_slug) ORDER BY strat;

enumeration_sql.label: C4_coverage_label_disagreement_rows
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- (C) ROWS TO FLAG FOR FOLLOW-UP (not interpolations): mula passages whose stratum lookup and whose external-coverage status disagree with neighbors in the same work (e.g. zero-coverage 1early suttas, or 2late suttas with high foreign breadth). Surface for human follow-up; do NOT auto-interpret.
SELECT p.work_slug, p.id, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat, char_length(p.original) AS rc,
       count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') AS foreign_edges,
       count(DISTINCT pp.parallel_lang) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') AS langs
FROM passages p LEFT JOIN passage_parallels pp ON pp.passage_id=p.id
WHERE p.work_role='mula' AND p.work_slug<>'pli-vism' AND p.is_primary=true
  AND stratum(p.work_slug) IN ('1early','2late')
GROUP BY p.work_slug, p.id
HAVING (stratum(p.work_slug)='1early' AND count(pp.*) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli')=0)
    OR (stratum(p.work_slug)='2late' AND count(DISTINCT pp.parallel_lang) FILTER (WHERE pp.parallel_lang<>'pli') >= 3)
ORDER BY p.work_slug, foreign_edges DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: CODE_pariyaya_sense_rows
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- POLYSEMY SENSE-AUDIT rows for COMM_METHOD: pariyāya is the lesson-of-%carita%; pull a blind sample of attha+mula hits with surrounding text for κ-coded sense split (exegetical-method/synonym vs discourse-mode/occasion) BEFORE any COMM_METHOD count is reported as 'systematization'.
SELECT p.id, p.work_role, p.work_slug, stratum(p.work_slug) AS strat,
       substring(p.original from '.{0,80}(\mpariyāy|\mpariyay).{0,80}') AS kwic
FROM passages p
WHERE p.is_primary=true AND p.original ~* '(\mpariyāy|\mpariyay)'
  AND p.work_role IN ('mula','attha')
ORDER BY random() LIMIT 200;

density_method: All magnitudes are per-character, never raw rows (T2). (A) comm_load = attha_chars/root_chars (a ratio, unitless, already length-normalized on both axes); par_density = foreign_par_edges / (root_chars/1e6) = foreign edges per root Mchar. Association tested per collection (DN/MN/SN/AN separately, NEVER pooled) as a partial Spearman rho of comm_load vs par_density controlling for (i) root tag_richness and (ii) a cataloguing-granularity proxy (distinct-sutta count of the parent work under SC, from C3). Lexicon densities (A3/A4) = hit_passages / layer-Mchar using the frozen denominators (mula 53.543921, attha 29.488533, tika 28.357746). (B) RetellShare = (retells+mentions)/all_edges per collection, reported raw AND within per-collection root-length terciles (B2) so the ordering is read length-controlled. (C) coverage = share of mula passages with >=1 foreign edge; density = foreign edges per stratum Mchar (denominator = SUM of distinct root char_length /1e6, deduped via is_primary to avoid CST/SC double-count); breadth = distinct parallel_lang count; relation-mix = per-relation_type shares. The PLI-only mirror (C2) uses identical math on parallel_lang='pli'. Effect sizes (rho, share gaps) carry bootstrap CIs; nothing is read as significant on point estimates alone.
negative_controls: LEAD CONTROL (C2): PLI-only intra-Pali coverage cartography. The early(1early)>late(2late) external-coverage ordering must NOT be reproduced by the pli-only sub-graph. If intra-Pali coverage shows the same gradient at comparable effect size, the (C) early>late 'coverage' signal is an SC cataloguing-completeness artifact, not differential cross-tradition transmission — H0-C wins and the chronology reading is withdrawn.

REGISTER CONTROL (A4): NEG_REGISTER_CONTROL lexicon (evaṃ me sutaṃ / tena samayena / atha kho / bhagavā) density vs par_density. These content-empty framing tokens must show NO association with parallel density beyond length. If par_density tracks generic register as strongly as it tracks COMM_METHOD/DHAMMA_EPITHET, the (A) systematization signal is a generic length/register artifact.

LENGTH CONTROL (B2): RetellShare computed within per-collection root-length terciles. If DN>MN>{SN,AN} only holds across the pooled set but vanishes inside matched length bins, the (B) gradient is a length artifact (partial H0-B).

GRANULARITY CONTROL (C3): SC-vs-CST distinct-work counts per stratum. A coverage gap that is really a cataloguing-granularity gap (CST coarse vagga rows vs SC fine sutta rows) is flagged, never reported as an interpolation or a transmission gap.
falsifiers: (A) FALSIFIED if, in any collection, partial Spearman(comm_load, par_density | tag_richness, granularity) has |rho|<0.15 with CI crossing 0 — OR if NEG_REGISTER (A4) correlates with par_density as strongly as DHAMMA_EPITHET/COMM_METHOD (effect is register/length, not centrality).
(A) ALSO FALSIFIED if the sign flips across collections (e.g. positive in DN, negative in SN): no coherent house-thesis direction survives.

(B) FALSIFIED if RetellShare does NOT order DN>MN>{SN,AN} in the raw pass (B1), OR if the ordering disappears inside matched length terciles (B2), OR if SN is NOT parallels-dominant (parallels_share not the highest / not >0.5 of edges).

(C) FALSIFIED (the decisive one) if the PLI-only control (C2) reproduces the early>late ordering at comparable effect size — the external-coverage gradient is then an SC cataloguing artifact, not transmission history, and H0-C is reported.
(C) ALSO FALSIFIED if foreign coverage does not exceed PLI coverage at all (graph is mostly intra-Pali), making the 'cross-tradition embedding' frame inapplicable.

WHOLE STUDY void if pariyāya sense-audit (CODE pass) shows COMM_METHOD hits are majority non-exegetical sense (discourse-mode/occasion), since the systematization content check would then be measuring narration, not commentary.
predicted_signal: PREDICTED (house thesis, to be confirmed): (A) per-collection partial Spearman rho in the +0.20..+0.45 band, strongest in MN/DN (heavily-glossed discourse collections), weaker but still positive in SN/AN; NEG_REGISTER flat (~0). (B) RetellShare ordering DN > MN > {SN, AN} with DN clearly highest and SN lowest RetellShare but highest parallels_share (SN parallels-dominant anchor, parallels_share plausibly >0.6). (C) foreign coverage 1early > 2late by a visible margin, with lang_breadth also higher for 1early; and CRITICALLY the PLI-only mirror NOT reproducing that ordering (pli_coverage roughly flat or reversed across strata). TO BE MEASURED (no prediction committed): absolute coverage/density magnitudes, exact rho values and CIs, the comm_load distribution shape, the SC-vs-CST granularity ratio, and the pariyāya exegetical-sense fraction. Whether C2 stays null is the load-bearing measured quantity — it decides H1-C vs H0-C.
primary_loci: DN 16 Mahāparinibbāna (dn16) — densely retold/mentioned, anchor for DN high RetellShare
DN 22 / MN 10 Satipaṭṭhāna (dn22, mn10) — high comm_load + heavy cross-tradition parallels, (A) high-load high-density exemplar
MN 1 Mūlapariyāya (mn1) — extreme attha_chars/root_chars (the Mūlapariyāya commentary is vast), (A) comm_load outlier + pariyāya sense-audit locus
SN 56.11 Dhammacakkappavattana (sn56.11) — parallels-dominant, SN anchor for verbatim 'parallels' edges
MN 141 Saccavibhaṅga / SN 56 Saccasaṃyutta — padabhājana/vibhaṅga analytic register, COMM_METHOD locus
AN formulaic nipāta suttas (an3/an4/an5...) — short pericope, low RetellShare / high parallels_share test
Dhamma-epithet formula loci (svākkhāto bhagavatā dhammo ... opanayiko paccattaṃ veditabbo viññūhi) — recurs across AN/SN, DHAMMA_EPITHET centrality probe
scope_limits: Cannot establish chronology or actual transmission HISTORY: stratum() is a work_slug lookup (T1), not independent dating; 'early/late' is a label, and any layer disagreement is never independent chronological evidence.

Cannot read foreign parallels' CONTENT: parallel_have=false for non-pli, the graph holds edges/relation_type/lang only, no foreign text — so 'parallel' here means a catalogued structural link, not a verified textual match.
Coverage is bounded by SuttaCentral mapping completeness: an absence of edges is absence-of-cataloguing, not proven absence-of-parallel; the C3 bound quantifies this but cannot remove it.

Cannot settle WHY a sutta is heavily glossed (doctrinal importance vs difficulty vs liturgical use) — comm_load measures attention, not motive; findings are corpus-linguistic, never doctrinal verdicts.

RetellShare depends on SC's relation_type labelling policy ('retells'/'mentions'/'parallels'); a systematic labelling bias by collection would confound (B) and is only partially guarded by length control.
comm_load mapping (A2) relies on slug-base matching mula to attha; multi-sutta or vagga-level commentary blocks attach coarsely and inject mapping noise, capped not eliminated.
Pooling across collections is forbidden (NEVER pool DN/MN/SN/AN); cross-collection comparisons are descriptive only, never a single combined statistic.
iaa_plan: Two blind coders independently sense-code the 200-row pariyāya KWIC sample (CODE_pariyaya_sense_rows) into {EXEGETICAL (method/synonym/word-analysis) | DISCOURSE-MODE (manner/way/occasion) | OTHER/AMBIGUOUS}, coders blind to passage stratum and work_role. Report Cohen's κ; require κ ≥ 0.67 before COMM_METHOD counts are used as a 'systematization' signal. If κ < 0.67, adjudicate a codebook revision on a fresh sample and re-run. SEPARATELY, the C4 coverage/label-disagreement rows are dual-reviewed for FOLLOW-UP triage only (flagged as 'investigate', explicitly NOT coded as interpolations or transmission gaps) — these are surfaced, not scored. relation_type itself is taken as-given from the SC graph (not re-coded), since it is an edge label that escapes the stratum-by-construction trap and re-coding it would require the foreign text we do not hold.
